originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&argc` is never used.

reasoning: Line 16: `p = *argv++, --argc` — the comma expression decrements argc and stores the new value back, but argc is never read afterward — dead store of argc. This statement is in main() outside any #ifdef, so the dead store occurs in all configurations. No '// ERROR' for argc dead store → new bug not in VBDB. PC requires UNREGISTER=T AND SCAN=T, but argc is dead regardless of these flags. PC is over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
